subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Carbon Cost of Cloud
description: Learn The Carbon Cost of Cloud in one minute.

hook.text: Data centers consume 1-2% of global electricity. As AI and cloud grow, that number is climbing fast.

buildup.text: A single data center can use as much electricity as a small city. Cooling alone accounts for 40% of energy consumption.

discovery.text: Cloud providers are investing billions in renewable energy: solar farms, wind power, and even nuclear partnerships.

twist.text: Moving to the cloud is often greener than on-premises. Shared infrastructure runs at higher utilization — less waste.

climax.text: AWS, Google, and Microsoft have pledged carbon neutrality. But growing demand may outpace renewable investments.

punchline.text: Cloud is greener than on-premises. But growing demand challenges that edge.

quiz.question: Why is moving to the cloud often greener than on-premises?
options: Shared cloud infrastructure runs at higher utilization, wasting less energy per workload
Cloud data centers don't use any electricity
On-premises servers are always powered by fossil fuels
